Looking at the code, I can see the issue. In the "Agent Tool Call" section (the chat messages area on the right side), when adding a tool call message, the `addToolCallMessage` function is called, and for in-built tools, it passes empty params `[]`:

```javascript
onSelectInbuiltTool={(
  toolId,
  toolName
) => {
  addToolCallMessage(
    toolId,
    toolName,
    []
  );
}}
```

And for custom tools:
```javascript
onSelectCustomTool={(tool, params) => {
  // Always add tool call directly with params (empty values if has params)
  addToolCallMessage(
    tool.uuid,
    tool.name,
    params.map((p) => ({
      name: p.name,
      value: "",
    }))
  );
}}
```

So the issue is that when adding a tool call in the chat messages (Agent Tool Call), the `ToolPicker` component's `onSelectCustomTool` passes parameters, but I need to verify if the `ToolPicker` is actually passing the correct params.

Let me check the `ToolPicker` component to see how it handles params:</details></think>

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Now I understand the issue. Looking at both files:

1. In the "Tool invocation test" section (left column), when a tool is selected via `addToolFromSelection`, the parameters are extracted from the tool's config and stored in `expectedParameters` state.

2. In the "Agent Tool Call" section (right column chat messages), when a tool is selected, `onSelectCustomTool` is called with the tool and its params. This is then passed to `addToolCallMessage`:

```javascript
onSelectCustomTool={(tool, params) => {
  // Always add tool call directly with params (empty values if has params)
  addToolCallMessage(
    tool.uuid,
    tool.name,
    params.map((p) => ({
      name: p.name,
      value: "",
    }))
  );
}}
```

The `ToolPicker` component correctly gets the params via `getToolParams(tool)` and passes them to the callback. So the params should be passed correctly.

But wait - looking at the screenshot, I see the issue. The user is showing:
- **Left side (Tool to test)**: Has `plan_next_question` selected with param `param1` shown under "Expected extracted parameters"
- **Right side (Agent Tool Call)**: Shows `plan_next_question` but no parameters are displayed

Let me look more closely at how the chat message displays the tool call. Looking at lines 1118-1170:

```jsx
{/* Tool Call Display */}
{message.role === "tool_call" && (
  <div className="w-1/2">
    <div className="bg-[#1a1a1a] border border-[#333] rounded-2xl p-4">
      <div className="flex items-center gap-2 mb-2">
        <svg>...</svg>
        <span className="text-sm font-medium text-orange-400">
          {message.toolName}
        </span>
      </div>
      {message.toolParams &&
        message.toolParams.length > 0 && (
          <div className="space-y-2 mt-3">
            {message.toolParams.map((param, idx) => (
              <div key={idx} className="flex items-center gap-2">
                <span className="text-xs text-gray-400 min-w-[80px]">
                  {param.name}:
                </span>
                <input
                  type="text"
                  value={param.value}
                  onChange={(e) =>
                    updateToolParam(message.id, idx, e.target.value)
                  }
                  className="flex-1 bg-[#2a2a2a] border border-[#444] rounded px-2 py-1 text-xs text-white"
                />
              </div>
            ))}
          </div>
        )}
    </div>
  </div>
)}
```

The display checks if `message.toolParams` exists and has length > 0. So the params should render if they're being stored in the message. The problem might be that `toolParams` isn't being set when the tool call message is created, or it's being set to an empty array. When loading existing tests, the tool call messages from history are being parsed, but the `toolParams` aren't being populated from the tool configuration. Looking at the history parsing logic, I can see that `toolParams` are being constructed from the parsed arguments, converting each entry into an object with `name` and `value` properties. The problem is that this approach only captures what's in the arguments—it doesn't fetch the full parameter definitions (like type, description, or validation rules) from the actual tool config, which is why the params might not display correctly in the UI. not just from the saved values. If the saved arguments are empty, the display still needs to show the parameter inputs based on the tool's actual schema.

I need to look up the tool by name in `availableTools` when loading from history and extract its parameter schema, then merge those with any saved argument values so fields appear even if empty. When adding a new tool call message, I should verify the `onSelectCustomTool` callback is passing the correct params through the ToolPicker component.

Looking at the callback implementation, it correctly maps the params from `getToolParams(tool)` into the message with empty string values. The real problem surfaces when loading an existing test—the saved `arguments` might be incomplete or missing, so I need to reconstruct the full parameter set from the tool's actual configuration rather than relying solely on what was persisted.

When the dialog opens and `initialConfig` gets processed, there's a race condition: the useEffect that fetches `availableTools` might not complete before the one handling `initialConfig` runs. I should add `availableTools` as a dependency to the second useEffect so that when a tool call appears in the history, I can look up its complete parameter schema from the loaded tools and merge any existing saved values with the full expected structure.</details></think>

Now I understand the issue. When loading an existing test, the tool call messages in the chat history are parsed using only the saved argument values from the API, not the actual tool parameters from the tool's configuration. If the saved arguments are empty, no parameter inputs are shown.

The fix needs to:
1. When parsing history with tool calls, look up the tool by name in `availableTools` to get its parameter schema
2. Merge any saved values with the tool's actual parameters

```javascript
} else if (initialConfig.evaluation.type === "tool_call") {
  const toolCalls = initialConfig.evaluation.tool_calls;

  // Check if tool_calls is empty array
  if (!toolCalls || toolCalls.length === 0) {
    setToolCallExpectation("should-call");
  } else {
    const toolCall = toolCalls[0];
    if (toolCall) {
      // Set selected tool
      setSelectedTool({
        id: toolCall.tool,
        name: toolCall.tool,
      });

      // Check is_called to determine expectation
      if (toolCall.is_called === false) {
        setToolCallExpectation("should-not-call");
        setAcceptAnyParameterValues(false);
      } else {
        // Set tool call expectation to "should-call"
        setToolCallExpectation("should-call");

        // Set accept_any_arguments - only true if explicitly set to true
        const acceptAny = toolCall.accept_any_arguments === true;
        setAcceptAnyParameterValues(acceptAny);

        // Load expected parameters if not accepting any
        if (
          !acceptAny &&
          toolCall.arguments &&
          Object.keys(toolCall.arguments).length > 0
        ) {
          setExpectedParameters(
            Object.entries(toolCall.arguments).map(
              ([name, value], idx) => ({
                id: `param-${idx}`,
                name,
                value: String(value),
              })
            )
          );
        }
      }
    }
  }
}
```

The same problem appears here—when loading a tool test configuration, it pulls parameters only from the saved `toolCall.arguments`. If arguments are empty or incomplete, the UI won't display the full parameter schema from the tool's definition. ```diff
@@ -239,4 +239,165 @@
      fetchTools();
    }, [isOpen]);
+ 
+   // Populate fields from initialConfig when editing an existing test
+   useEffect(() =&gt; {
+     if (initialConfig &amp;&amp; !availableToolsLoading) {
+       // Parse history and convert to chatMessages format
+       if (initialConfig.history &amp;&amp; initialConfig.history.length &gt; 0) {
+         const messages: Array&lt;{
+           id: string;
+           role: "agent" | "user" | "tool_call";
+           content: string;
+           toolName?: string;
+           toolId?: string;
+           toolParams?: Array&lt;{ name: string; value: string }&gt;;
+         }&gt; = [];
+ 
+         initialConfig.history.forEach((historyItem, index) =&gt; {
+           if (historyItem.role === "assistant") {
+             if (historyItem.tool_calls &amp;&amp; historyItem.tool_calls.length &gt; 0) {
+               // This is a tool call message
+               const toolCall = historyItem.tool_calls[0];
+               let parsedArgs: Record&lt;string, any&gt; = {};
+               try {
+                 parsedArgs = JSON.parse(toolCall.function.arguments);
+               } catch {
+                 parsedArgs = {};
+               }
+ 
+               // Look up the tool in availableTools to get full parameter list
+               const foundTool = availableTools.find(
+                 (t) =&gt; t.name === toolCall.function.name
+               );
+               let toolParams: Array&lt;{ name: string; value: string }&gt; = [];
+ 
+               if (foundTool) {
+                 // Get params from tool config
+                 const configParams = getToolParams(foundTool);
+                 // Merge with saved values
+                 toolParams = configParams.map((p) =&gt; ({
+                   name: p.name,
+                   value:
+                     parsedArgs[p.name] !== undefined
+                       ? String(parsedArgs[p.name])
+                       : "",
+                 }));
+               } else {
+                 // Fallback to saved args only if tool not found
+                 toolParams = Object.entries(parsedArgs).map(([name, value]) =&gt; ({
+                   name,
+                   value: String(value),
+                 }));
+               }
+ 
+               messages.push({
+                 id: toolCall.id || &#96;tool-${index}&#96;,
+                 role: "tool_call",
+                 content: "",
+                 toolId: toolCall.id,
+                 toolName: toolCall.function.name,
+                 toolParams,
+               });
+             } else {
+               // Regular assistant message
+               messages.push({
+                 id: &#96;msg-${index}&#96;,
+                 role: "agent",
+                 content: historyItem.content || "",
+               });
+             }
+           } else if (historyItem.role === "user") {
+             messages.push({
+               id: &#96;msg-${index}&#96;,
+               role: "user",
+               content: historyItem.content || "",
+             });
+           }
+           // Skip "tool" role messages as they are tool responses
+         });
+ 
+         if (messages.length &gt; 0) {
+           setChatMessages(messages);
+         }
+       }
+ 
+       // Populate evaluation fields
+       if (initialConfig.evaluation) {
+         if (
+           initialConfig.evaluation.type === "response" &amp;&amp;
+           initialConfig.evaluation.criteria
+         ) {
+           setExpectedMessage(initialConfig.evaluation.criteria);
+         } else if (initialConfig.evaluation.type === "tool_call") {
+           const toolCalls = initialConfig.evaluation.tool_calls;
+ 
+           // Check if tool_calls is empty array
+           if (!toolCalls || toolCalls.length === 0) {
+             setToolCallExpectation("should-call");
+           } else {
+             const toolCall = toolCalls[0];
+             if (toolCall) {
+               // Look up the tool in availableTools to get full parameter list
+               const foundTool = availableTools.find(
+                 (t) =&gt; t.name === toolCall.tool || t.uuid === toolCall.tool
+               );
+ 
+               // Set selected tool
+               setSelectedTool({
+                 id: foundTool?.uuid || toolCall.tool,
+                 name: toolCall.tool,
+               });
+ 
+               // Check is_called to determine expectation
+               if (toolCall.is_called === false) {
+                 setToolCallExpectation("should-not-call");
+                 setAcceptAnyParameterValues(false);
+               } else {
+                 // Set tool call expectation to "should-call"
+                 setToolCallExpectation("should-call");
+ 
+                 // Set accept_any_arguments - only true if explicitly set to true
+                 const acceptAny = toolCall.accept_any_arguments === true;
+                 setAcceptAnyParameterValues(acceptAny);
+ 
+                 // Load expected parameters - merge tool config with saved values
+                 if (!acceptAny) {
+                   if (foundTool) {
+                     // Get params from tool config
+                     const configParams = getToolParams(foundTool);
+                     // Merge with saved values
+                     setExpectedParameters(
+                       configParams.map((p, idx) =&gt; ({
+                         id: &#96;param-${idx}&#96;,
+                         name: p.name,
+                         value:
+                           toolCall.arguments?.[p.name] !== undefined
+                             ? String(toolCall.arguments[p.name])
+                             : "",
+                       }))
+                     );
+                   } else if (
+                     toolCall.arguments &amp;&amp;
+                     Object.keys(toolCall.arguments).length &gt; 0
+                   ) {
+                     // Fallback to saved args only if tool not found
+                     setExpectedParameters(
+                       Object.entries(toolCall.arguments).map(
+                         ([name, value], idx) =&gt; ({
+                           id: &#96;param-${idx}&#96;,
+                           name,
+                           value: String(value),
+                         })
+                       )
+                     );
+                   }
+                 }
+               }
+             }
+           }
+         }
+       }
+     }
+   }, [initialConfig, availableTools, availableToolsLoading]);
